text:
Nen Sense (ヒソカの, Hisoka no Jōken—lit. "Hisoka's Terms") is the 6 th volume of the Hunter × Hunter manga series. It was released by Shueisha on October 4 th , 1999 in Japan and by Viz Media on January 3 rd , 2006 in English.

Chapters

45. Ren (レン, Ren)
46. Nen (ネン, Nen)
47. The Invisible Wall (えない, Mienai Kabe)
48. Hisoka's Terms (ヒソカの, Hisoka no Jōken)
49. The Battle Begins!! (！！, Sentō Kaishi!!)
50. Zetsu (ゼツ, Zetsu)
51. Ten (, Ten)
52. Kastro (カストロ, Kasutoro)
53. Double (ダブル, Daburu)
54. Cause of Defeat (, Haiin)

Summary

While working their way up Heavens Arena, Gon and Killua eventually meet another strong young boy, Zushi, and his master, Wing. Zushi is able to use a strange energy, similar to what Killua's brother Illumi can use. Although they ask Wing to teach them the secret to this energy, Wing refuses to since they are not his students. Upon reaching the 200 th floor of the tower, Gon and Killua discover Hisoka waiting for them, having tracked their movements through their airship ticket purchases. He prevents Gon and Killua from progressing to the 200 th floor with a flood of harmful energy that they can feel but not see. Behind them, Wing appears and stops them from trying to pass Hisoka, agreeing to teach them the secret of that energy, which is called Nen.

In under four hours, Wing gives Gon and Killua a crash course in the very basics of Nen—the control of spiritual energy existing inside all beings—starting them out with a boost of energy from his own body. They return to the tower and, using their Nen to protect themselves from Hisoka's negative energy, register for the 200 th floor. Hisoka is satisfied that his promising playthings will survive there, and gives Gon a condition: if he wins at least one fight, he will agree to fight him.

Gon immediately registers for a match to test out his new strength, but he will almost certainly lose. He is placed in a match against Gido, and although he struggles valiantly, he loses the match and ends up with a broken arm. Soon after, Hisoka shows off his skills in a match against Kastro. Although Kastro seems to have the upper hand through most of the match, Hisoka is supremely confident, pulling off several tricks that seem like magic and leaving Kastro a ragged corpse filled with playing cards on the floor of the arena.
